arguments.task: Visit the official Salesforce Commerce developer documentation and related official Salesforce pages. Extract evidence for: product description, available APIs and whether they are REST or GraphQL, authentication methods for Salesforce Commerce Cloud APIs, any access/pricing or account gating requirements for using the APIs or developer center, any rate limit notes, and whether Salesforce provides or documents an MCP server for Commerce Cloud. Prefer official developer docs, API docs, auth docs, release notes, pricing/access pages, help docs, and official GitHub pages. Return the exact page URLs used and concise quoted findings.

input.thought: Start focused browser research on official Salesforce Commerce Cloud sources.

raw_result.description: Salesforce Commerce Cloud is Salesforce’s digital commerce platform for building B2C, B2B, and D2C shopping experiences and integrations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Commerce API docs indicate OAuth-based access via Account Manager and Shopper Login and API Access Service (SLAS); docs also reference JWT-based shopper authentication flows. Exact auth coverage across all Commerce Cloud surfaces was not fully verified from completed task output.
raw_result.gating_reason: The gathered official sources were Salesforce developer and product pages for Commerce Cloud, which is an enterprise Salesforce product rather than a clearly self-serve API signup flow; evidence gathered did not verify a self-serve free/trial path for production Commerce Cloud API access.

api_surface.breadth: Official Salesforce Commerce developer docs show a broad commerce API surface spanning Commerce API/SCAPI references and platform overviews for shopper, admin, and commerce workflows across B2C and broader commerce offerings; evidence suggests both REST and GraphQL are present, but exact scope by product line remains partially unverified.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Salesforce Commerce Cloud MCP server evidence was confirmed from the gathered sources. A GitHub org search was initiated but not completed to a verified MCP listing.

raw_result.blocker: Enterprise/product-access gating and incomplete verification of self-serve provisioning details for Commerce Cloud environments make initial toolkit setup likely require existing Salesforce Commerce access or manual account setup.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Rate-limit/governance documentation was not successfully reached in the gathered run; no verified numeric limits should be asserted from current evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer docs clearly exist under Salesforce Commerce, including Commerce API overview and shopper customer references. Gathered browser evidence specifically noted SLAS and JWT-based authentication references on shopper-facing API docs, indicating agent-usable API surfaces exist today.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Several fields remain partially ambiguous from the completed evidence set: whether Commerce APIs are definitively both REST and GraphQL across the assigned product scope, exact self-serve vs sales-gated access tier, verified rate-limit documentation, and whether any official MCP server exists.
